rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., the contradiction between paired outcomes and doubly-robust framing), and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, such as the need to verify empirical numbers and the precise success metric.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set of dimensions without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., overclaims and logical inconsistencies) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, such as the corrections to the optimality claim and the addition of methodological caveats.
